derivation_note: Inherited verbatim from plate_calibrations/transit-announcement-hub (Cycle 18 agent calibration, verified by overlay inspection). Location-level calibration; Issue-02 panels using this plate inherit it. Ground-plane values measured with the estimate-then-inspect discipline: horizon solved programmatically from detected floor tile joints (see horizon_derivation), calibration object and light positions sampled from labeled 4x/3x grid zooms of the plate, then verified against the calibrate_check.py overlay.

calibration.note: Right turnstile pedestal: top rim y=383, foot y=475 sampled from a 4x grid zoom. A transit turnstile is ~1.0 m, i.e. door-handle/waist scale -- right for a chibi character, unlike the human-height vending machine or the doorway. Scale sanity: h/(y_foot - horizon) = 92/126 = 0.73 of camera eye height, consistent with a ~1.0 m barrier seen from standing eye level.

ground_plane.horizon_derivation: MEASURED: Canny + probabilistic Hough on the floor region (y 480-720) detected 21 receding tile-joint segments; pairwise intersections of opposite-slope joints converge tightly at a vanishing point x=629, y=349 (IQR of intersection y: 348-351). Verified visually by rendering every detected joint extended across the plate -- all converge mid center departure board (one-point perspective). Horizon adopted at y=349; back-wall floor join at y~468 sits below it as expected.

light_sources.note: Three large luminous departure boards on the back wall are the dominant light source; their teal-cyan glow reads across the whole glossy floor and is the main reflection everywhere a character will stand.
Magenta neon ceiling tube top-center (twin smaller magenta tubes upper-left add the same hue from screen-left); gives the magenta rim/fill from above.

Positioned on the long right vertical trim bar; represents the full set of cyan neon segments around the right doorway arch, which give strong cyan fill and floor reflections on the right third of the plate. True hue is saturated cyan, not white -- named with the listed color so the compositor does not fall back to neutral grey.

weather.note: Indoor transit hall: rain is visible through the upper-right skylight and as leak-drip streaks on the back wall; ceiling leaks pool as mirror puddles on the polished tile floor.

reflective_surfaces.note: The hall floor is polished glossy tile -- the departure-board glow reflects across all of it, so any placed character needs a soft floor reflection wherever they stand. Bounded by the back-wall join (y~468) and the right doorway base line; the lower-left bench block (three benches, diagonal band from ~(355,525) to ~(200,719)) is cut out because the benches occlude the floor there and reflections must not be painted over them.
Large mirror puddle center-left foreground; hard cyan board reflection.
Largest mirror puddle bottom-right, runs off the bottom edge; reflects the cyan doorway trim.
Elongated puddle just past the mid-floor hazard stripe, screen right.
Small puddle near the back wall, left of center.

camera.angle: eye-level, one-point perspective square onto the departure boards, static locked-off

decompression_note: Was MZ-2026-09-02_P02_PANEL03 in the 24-panel edition; same locked beat, renumbered by the 16-page/96-panel format directive.
